episode0.title: FIRST CONTACT — LIFELINE CINEMATIC PASS V2

puzzle0.title: RELAY ACCESS
puzzle0.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization, not the deprecated fragment.

puzzle1.title: RELAY POWER ROUTING
puzzle1.description: Legacy matching puzzle preserved for compatibility. V5 replaces this with a two-choice relay power decision.

event5.text: Please tell me that went somewhere.

event19.text: My name is Ethan Hale. ECHO Station. Chief engineer.

event21.text: I'm jammed into a service alcove beside a relay corridor.

event23.text: Emergency strip on the floor. No ceiling lights.

event25.text: Something behind the wall keeps ticking.

event28.text: It stopped when I typed that.

event5.text: Which currently means 'nearest idiot to a broken panel.'

event7.text: I used to keep the relay spine and life-support buses from eating each other.

event5.text: I'm sending suit telemetry.

event7.text: If I'm fake, I'm a fake with extremely committed lung problems.
event7.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
event7.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
event7.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

event15.text: The suit sounds way too proud of that number.

event5.text: Lights went first.

event9.text: Then the relay started throwing old voices back at me.

event11.text: Loops. Distress calls. People who stopped needing help months ago.

event14.text: You are the first response that feels like a person.

event4.text: I needed to hear that more than I want to admit.

event8.text: I'm tired, low on air, and my memory has holes in it.

event10.text: If I sound too certain, make me prove it.

event3.text: Cable trays on the left wall.

event5.text: Some melted clean through their brackets.

event9.text: My boots are cutting fresh tracks through it.

event17.text: That's not my favorite discovery today.

event1.text: Relay room should be past the dark stretch.

event5.text: I can pull one damaged crew record from the cache.

event4.title: CREW LOG: ETHAN HALE
event4.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE — CHIEF ENGINEER
RELAY MAINTENANCE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
STATUS: ACTIVE

NOTE: Secondary maintenance index lists deprecated fragment: _319.
Current authorization must be reconstructed from active record.
event4.caption: Recovered identity and maintenance fragment. Authorization appears partially damaged.

event12.text: Do not copy the old fragment whole. It is marked deprecated, and stations usually mean that in the least friendly way.

event14.text: I'm going to the relay door.

event16.text: Both hands for the corridor. If I keep talking, I'll stop listening.

event3.text: Took longer than it should have.

event5.text: Half the corridor is blocked by insulation panels.

event7.text: The ticking stopped when I passed the third service hatch.

event12.text: Panel is alive. Keypad is cracked, but the first four digits still respond.

event6.text: Do not try another random number.

event10.text: I can hear the lock cycling inside the frame.

event19.text: Only one digit fits the damaged spot.

choice1.text: SLOW DOWN. WALK ME THROUGH IT.

event5.text: The current code isn't a riddle. It's just damaged.

event15.text: System says not to use the whole old record.

event18.text: So we steal the part that fits.

event3.text: I could also lick the panel and hope it respects confidence.

event5.text: Use the record.

event7.text: My memory is not better than evidence right now.

event5.text: Oh.

event7.text: The room is colder than the corridor.

event15.text: One pulsing like it borrowed a heartbeat and installed it wrong.

event17.text: Reserve power is still tied to life support.

event22.text: If I clean the carrier, I have to pull from reserve.

event24.text: If I leave reserve alone, the signal stays ugly.

event5.text: Breathing remains fashionable.

event7.text: I'll force the relay through the dirty line.

event11.text: Awful is still better than dead.

event5.text: My suit immediately hated that.

event12.text: Just saying the quiet part out loud before the alarms do.

event5.text: Relay is holding.

event9.text: Floor kicks every time the dirty line surges.

event11.text: Feels like standing on a sleeping animal.

event15.text: My suit alarm finally stopped scolding me.

event17.text: There's a security cache behind the second relay column.

event22.text: I do not remember why looking at it makes my stomach drop.

event5.text: Signal is clearer now.

event7.text: I can hear the silence between your responses.

event9.text: That should not feel comforting, but here we are.

event11.text: Suit alarm hated the power dip.

event13.text: It made a sound I can only describe as disappointed in me personally.

event15.text: There's a security cache behind the second relay column.

event20.text: That memory comes with a bad taste.

event1.text: Cache is behind the second relay stack.

event6.text: Relay console is on the other side of the room.

event8.text: If I leave it alone, the carrier may drift.

event10.text: The cache panel is under a burned insulation flap.

event12.text: I can see the edge from here.

event15.text: Door Twelve is written on the label.

event17.text: I hate that I recognize my own handwriting.

event5.text: If the line warbles, don't panic.

event7.text: Actually, panic quietly. I need both hands.

event5.text: I was hoping you missed that.

event7.text: Something is sitting on the edge of the carrier.

event13.text: Like someone behind you trying not to breathe.

event23.text: Before it decides what we're allowed to know.

event5.text: Had to pry the panel with a broken bracket.

event9.text: There's a sealed evidence packet inside.

event14.text: My hand went cold when I read it.

event5.text: Should not be warm.

event7.text: Something scratched the inside of the insulation flap.

event12.text: I don't know how something scratches from inside a wall panel.

event1.title: SECURITY LOG
event1.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
event1.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

event3.title: MEDICAL REPORT
event3.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
event3.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

event4.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
event4.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
event4.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
event4.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choice1.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

event1.text: I can't explain something I don't remember doing.

event5.text: I know exactly how weak it is.

event8.text: But if I make up a cleaner answer, we get a nicer lie.

event10.text: And a worse chance of living through it.

event5.text: Someone behind me shouting my name.

event12.text: My hand was near the override.

backgroundEvent0.text: ASK HIM WHAT HAPPENED AFTER THE DOOR OPENED

event5.text: The channel just pulled away from me.

event9.text: Something pushed into the carrier while we were reading.

event11.text: The evidence packet is still open.

event13.text: I need to seal it before the cache corrupts.

event15.text: Operator, if Door Twelve comes up again...

event17.text: Do not let me step around it.

event19.text: I don't trust my memory enough to protect either of us.

event10.text: Curiosity feels like courage until something answers back.

event15.text: Before it learns anything else from us.
